# game-omni — per-node output-criteria fixture
_The human-judged QUALITY bar a Hermes node-validation loop judges each node's artifact against (the complement to the mechanical Output Contract: the contract checks the artifact EXISTS, these criteria say whether it is GOOD). NEVER injected into a producing node's prompt — that would teach-to-the-test and void the clean-room signal. Last updated: 2026-06-18 — **chrome-CONSUMPTION vocab migration**: the built game now consumes the chrome via `gameConfig.shell`/`gameConfig.guidance` SECTIONS (`hud`/`coaching` RETIRED as top-level keys; uniform vocab = shell/guidance). The Shell/Guidance Artifact lines + the W2 block now read `gameConfig.shell.hud` / `gameConfig.guidance.coaching`; the rows still attributing `blueprint.hud[]`/`coaching[]` PRODUCTION to HARDEN/W1 are the DEFERRED harden↔chrome producer reconcile (a separate thread). PRIOR 2026-06-17 — **`action_3d` (the 2nd 3D substrate, WIN-LOSE) registered** — the W0 archetype list gained `action_3d` and a per-archetype `action_3d` judging block was ADDED at the tail (Harden / W2 / W4 entries) framing the `layout.actionWorld` → `world1.json` materialization bar (isomorphic to the voxel block, but win-lose: every WIN-LOSE node entry above still holds in full — only the actionWorld materialization is archetype-specific). PRIOR same day: the producing tier gained two CHROME-tier producing nodes (the dependency-separation reconstruction, `docs/reconstruction/contracts-v1.md` Contracts 2/3/4/6): **Guidance** (`author-guidance` — owns `blueprint.guidance` coaching[]/overlays[]/worldCues[], the in-game teaching layer bound to the published gameplay surface; cues RETRIEVE-resolve against `templates/core/guidance.json`) and **Shell** (`author-shell` — owns `blueprint.shell` hud[]/intro/sceneFlow/modes, the player-facing chrome projected from GDD-declared fields, parallel to gameplay; widgets/scenes RETRIEVE-resolve against `templates/core/shell.json`). A per-node entry for each was ADDED to the win-lose producing-node block above (Guidance: 8 acceptance criteria + 7 red flags; Shell: 8 acceptance criteria + 7 red flags), each carrying the explicit "RETRIEVE resolves against its per-node JSON" criterion (Contract 6 invariant 5/6). (PRIOR 2026-06-15 — the system gained the `voxel_sandbox` 3D substrate (category #14) + archetype-AGNOSTIC producing nodes + the `meta.goalModel` axis (`win-lose` default vs `open-ended`). W0's archetype is now resolved LIVE from `genres.json archetypes[]` (the closed enum was removed; the criteria above are updated). Each producing node's existing entry remains the WIN-LOSE bar; a NEW per-node "voxel / open-ended sandbox case" block was ADDED at the tail (W1 / Harden / W2 / W4 / VERIFY-2) framing what a good open-ended-sandbox artifact looks like — a JUDGING fixture for the human eye + the DEFERRED open-ended verify pass, never an instruction injected into a node.) (PRIOR 2026-06-14 — the asset pipeline was REFRAMED: the single W3 Assets entry SPLIT into a NEW **W3a Art Direction** (judges asset-prompts.json: palette + one creative prompt per non-audio slot, subject-only-its-own, the anti-theme-bleed bar) and an updated **W3b Assets** (real generation MANDATORY — no placeholder fallback; uniform ~CONTENT_FILL sizing; ar_flag flagged not distorted). Also 2026-06-14 — the DESIGN SIDE was SPLIT: **W1 Spec** now emits ONE markdown `spec/gdd.md` (reason-first PROSE + a single fenced-JSON milestones tail — NOT strict gdd.json+PLAN.md; strict JSON for the design body is a format tax that degrades reasoning and reads no better downstream); a NEW **Harden** producer node (`harden-blueprint`) PRODUCES `spec/blueprint.json`, the strict-JSON machine boundary the whole build binds to; and **VERIFY-1** became a PURE verifier — it READS the produced blueprint, RE-DERIVES the proofs, writes `spec/DESIGN_REVIEW.md`, applies a bounded STRENGTHEN-only stabilize-edit, and CREATES NOTHING the build binds to (the verify-node law). Earlier (2026-06-13): sharpened the BUILD nodes for the data-driven build side (W2 materializes src/levels/<level>.json + projects gameConfig.hud; W4 authors ONLY the custom[] delta, no coordinate in a .ts) and the asset-completeness no-cheap rule (a real asset per primary visible KIND within meta.assetBudget).)_

## W0 Classify (w0-classify)
**Artifact:** spec/classification.json
**Purpose:** First node in the chain; W1 Spec reads it to elaborate the core loop into milestones, W2 Scaffold routes the template lane off `archetype` (resolved LIVE from `templates/genres.json archetypes[]` — the registry, no hard-coded enum; the genre record's `coreBase`/`runtimeShape`/`configGroup` then drive the build), and the scopeCut is a hard contract every downstream node (W1's milestone sizing, W4's build) treats as out-of-bounds. A wrong archetype routes the entire pipeline to the wrong template; a loose/ambiguous core loop or thin scope-cut lets W4 sprawl into slop with no way to recover, since the design is frozen at VERIFY-1.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- archetype is byte-identical to an entry of `templates/genres.json archetypes[]` (the LIVE registry — currently {platformer, top_down, grid_logic, tower_defense, ui_heavy, voxel_sandbox, action_3d}; read it live, never assume a fixed list) and is justified by PHYSICS not by the prompt's genre word — the physicsProfile (hasGravity/perspective/movementType) is internally consistent with the chosen archetype (e.g. gravity:true ⇒ side ⇒ platformer; movement grid ⇒ grid_logic; first-person 3D build-and-mine ⇒ voxel_sandbox; third-person/high-angle 3D walk-and-shoot-a-city ⇒ action_3d), and reasoning names the specific KEY QUESTION / tie-break rule that decided it
- coreLoop is ONE self-enclosed present-tense sentence carrying the player verb + win-state + obstacle + fail-state (the loop returns to its start), describing the moment-to-moment LOOP — not a feature list, not multi-clause progression/menu enumeration
- coreVerb is a single concrete player action that is the spine of coreLoop AND matches a native mechanic of the chosen archetype's template (the prompt's main action is carried by the template, not pushed to scope-cut)
- scoringModel is exactly one of {none, bounded-collectible, bounded-threshold, performance} derived from the prompt's GOAL-TYPE, not the archetype: a completion prompt routes to none or bounded-collectible (never a reflexive 'performance' open counter), a survival/how-high/how-long prompt routes to performance, an explicit target/wave/opponent routes to bounded-threshold
- scopeCut is a non-empty list of ~4-8 short, GENERAL noun phrases naming SYSTEMS deliberately out of v1 (multiplayer, save/load, procedural gen, economy, story, mobile/touch/3D, AND multiple levels/stages — round one is one rich level by default) — never the juice/game-feel, never the core contested decision, and never a single named feature unique to this one prompt; it does NOT pre-cut a level sequence the prompt EXPLICITLY asked for
- mustPreserve names the ONE core contested decision (a risk weighed against a reward on the critical path) the cut must keep, as a relation not a per-prompt constant — so W1 cannot gut round one into a tutorial; it is present unless the prompt is a genuine no-threat sandbox
- prompt field is the raw args.prompt copied verbatim; confidence is high/medium/low and tracks actual ambiguity (high only when one archetype is unambiguous, medium when a tie-break/runner-up was used, low when the prompt barely fits or was empty/gibberish — with the mismatch named in reasoning)
- any unsupported request (3D, multiplayer, non-2D-web genre) is ABSORBED into scopeCut with the closest 2D archetype chosen, rather than the node refusing or emitting an out-of-set archetype
- the file is strict-valid JSON against classification.schema.json: all required fields present and correctly typed, no trailing/inline comments, no extra files written
**Red flags:**
- archetype chosen from the genre word the user typed rather than physics — the classic traps: Terraria/Angry Birds/Hill Climb labeled top_down or 'puzzle' when gravity ⇒ platformer; SimCity/Factorio as top_down when grid-building ⇒ grid_logic; Snake/Tetris/2048 as 'continuous' when discrete steps ⇒ grid_logic
- physicsProfile contradicts the archetype or the reasoning (e.g. archetype platformer but hasGravity:false, or movementType:grid with archetype top_down) — the structured physics doesn't justify the routing
- coreLoop is a feature/content list or multi-clause spec ('a platformer with 5 levels, a shop, boss fights, unlockable skins') instead of one self-enclosed loop sentence; or it omits the fail-state/win-state so W1 can't seed win/lose conditions
- scopeCut is empty, trivially short, cuts the juice/feedback on the core verb, cuts the core contested decision (gutting round one into a tutorial), pre-cuts a level sequence the prompt EXPLICITLY requested, or lists one prompt-specific named feature instead of a general system (a cut that helps only this one prompt is a bug)
- scoringModel reflexively set to 'performance' (an open counter) on a completion prompt, or contradicts the goal-type (a pure-puzzle/solve prompt scored as performance; a survival/how-long prompt scored as none) — the score-meaning bug this signal exists to prevent; or mustPreserve omitted/trivial on a prompt that clearly has a contested decision, leaving W1 free to cut round one to a tutorial
- confidence:high asserted on a genuinely ambiguous prompt with no tie-break disclosed, OR an empty/gibberish prompt classified to a confident specific game instead of defaulting to platformer/low with the fallback noted in reasoning

## W1 Spec (w1-spec)
**Artifact:** spec/gdd.md (ONE markdown design doc — merged GDD + playability plan as reason-first PROSE, ending in a single fenced ```json milestones tail)
**Purpose:** Harden and VERIFY-1 are the DOWNSTREAM READERS of gdd.md — reasoning nodes that read prose fine: Harden formalizes this design (its prose body + the milestone tail) into the strict frozen blueprint.json, and VERIFY-1 judges that blueprint. W2 (via the blueprint) scaffolds + freezes the subject-free meta.artStyle + the SUBJECT-line asset descriptions W3a authors prompts from; W3a/W3b fan over the resulting asset slots; W4 builds each milestone VERBATIM and populates window.__GAME__; VERIFY-2 replays the milestone assertions as the oracle. Because the milestone tail's assertions ARE the executable verification spine Harden formalizes and the whole downstream pipeline is frozen against, any unobservable, un-winnable, or status-incoherent assertion here propagates as a baked-in defect no later node can repair. The design BODY is prose (the reasoning medium); ONLY the milestones tail is strict JSON (the parseable spine Harden + the driver consume).
**Acceptance criteria:**
- FORMAT — the design BODY is reason-first PROSE (sections of plain markdown — entities, mechanics, controls, win/lose, scoring, the playability/level plan), NOT strict JSON: strict JSON for the design body is a FORMAT TAX that degrades the producer's reasoning (and is read no better by Harden/VERIFY-1, both reasoning nodes) — strict JSON is reserved for blueprint.json at Harden. The file ENDS in EXACTLY ONE fenced ```json block carrying ONLY { milestones: [...] } and nothing else (no GDD object, no prose after it), parseable by the driver and Harden in one shot.
- The prose body states meta CONSISTENT with classification.json (archetype/coreLoop/coreVerb/physicsProfile carried verbatim, the player named as the primary entity) and declares the subject-free artStyle + the per-asset SUBJECT lines W3a needs — no field contradicting classification.
- Every entity behavior, mechanic capability, control input, and config-tuning number named in the prose maps to a real capability of the chosen archetype's template (the §2 fingerprint) honoring its registry ROLES, and respects classification.scopeCut — nothing the template cannot natively do; compose-first (config a builtin → compose registered ids → a $custom gap named ONLY for a genuine novelty); config is flat game-specific tuning (no screenSize/renderConfig/debugConfig).
- STANDARD REACTIONS ARE BOUND TO REGISTERED LOGICS (the 2026-06-14 kind=system fix; the p02 unwired-mechanic defect): every mechanic that touches `score`, drives the win, or fails the player on contact binds to a registered logic id — collect→score ⇒ `CollectScore`, score≥N/all-collected→win ⇒ `ScoreGateGoal`, reach-exit (no score)→win ⇒ `GoalReach`, contact→back-to-start (failModel respawn/lives) ⇒ `ContactRespawn` on the enemy — OR a declared `custom[]` gap with an OBSERVABLE contract. A standard reaction bound only to a bare SCENE-HOOK id (`setupCustomCollisions`/`onLevelComplete`/`onPlayerDeath`) or to nothing is UNWIRED (a scene-hook is an empty override SEAM the logic CALLS, not a logic) — W4 then HALTs.
- The milestones tail holds 3-5 milestones in build order; M1 makes the core verb playable in a SAFE onboarding setting (the verb's observable changes with no fail-state triggered), and the FINAL milestone has a REACHABLE terminal end-state (a win that drives status->'won').
- Every milestone assertion in the tail follows the Given(setup)→When(input)→Then(observe+expect) grammar over window.__GAME__ (player.{x,y,vx,vy,health}, score, status, entities.count, archetype extras), never implementation calls; exactly ONE expect comparator per assertion; input.key drawn ONLY from controls[]; acceptance criteria and assertions 1:1 with no orphans.
- The status model is coherent: status:'won'/'lost' are terminal sinks with no edge out except reboot — a recoverable catch/fall/respawn keeps status:'playing' and observes a DISTINCT signal (lives decrement, or player returning to spawn coords), and the loseCondition prose matches that model rather than misusing the terminal status for a soft-fail.
- The design picks EXACTLY ONE fail-model and states it machine-readably (failModel ∈ {health,lives,respawn,none}) agreeing with the loseCondition observable; it does NOT leave maxHealth as inert tuning a HUD latches onto — the missing twin of the status-model rule (the frog1 hud-healthbar defect).
- The SCORE-MEANING contract holds (§3.6): the scoringModel carried from classification; if != none, a finite reachable maxScore == Σ of reward values is stated, every reward is marked idempotent (one-shot + respawn-safe), the readout prefers X/maxScore, the score gates/measures the win, and the rewards-milestone carries the two score assertions (a re-overlap incl. after a respawn observes score unchanged; score atMost maxScore); if scoringModel == none, NO score is declared and no score readout is surfaced (no vestigial counter).
- The RICHNESS + DIFFICULTY FLOOR holds (§3.5/§3.7): round one ELABORATES the prompt's THEME into a SUBSTANTIAL single-level game rather than placing the literally-named entities in a row on one screen. The enumerated inventory is present in the prose — a LONG critical path of DISTINCT escalating beats; the core threat contesting the path REPEATEDLY (more instances / faster / re-placed, ON the route, never parked in a corner); MULTIPLE genuine risk-vs-reward decisions (carrying classification.mustPreserve); RISING difficulty across the length (later beats measurably harder via the per-archetype elaboration levers — wider gaps / tighter timing / more & faster threats / denser waves / longer solve); an EARNED climax; the M1 teach as only the OPENING FRACTION. The coverage floor is met as a countable RELATION, never a genre constant: >=3 DISTINCT escalating challenge beats on the critical path (the teach + >=2 more, harder); difficulty rises (later beats measurably harder than the teach); the level is well beyond a single-screen ~30-second crossing. Richness = depth/length/escalation of the SAME loop (more instances of the named mechanics), NEVER a new SYSTEM the scope-cut forbids (no shop/economy/extra-enemy-TYPE feature creep). The §3.7 self-critique was applied (a thin draft was elaborated before return). A single rich level is the DEFAULT and correct (not a deficiency).
- A SINGLE rich level is the DEFAULT: the prose Level Ladder records LEVEL_ORDER: ['Level1Scene'] with escalation WITHIN the level (§3.5), and the 3-5 milestones decompose that ONE rich level (NOT one-level-per-milestone). A multi-level ladder is OPTIONAL and ON-DEMAND — declared ONLY if the prompt explicitly asked for multiple levels/stages, in which case it maps onto LevelManager.LEVEL_ORDER and each later level escalates via config + placement + ≤1 small capability (NOT new code), within the ≤5 milestone ceiling and the scope-cut. VIEWPORT vs WORLD (§4.6): the VIEWPORT is the template screenSize (read from the template gameConfig.screenSize, currently 432×768 portrait, full-bleed via Scale.FIT); the WORLD (layout.bounds) is the viewport for a single-screen level OR a MULTIPLE of the 432-wide viewport for a longer scrolling level (e.g. width = N×432, camera follows the player, world height normally 768) — a long §3.5 path implies a wide world, never capped at one screen, never a free 960×540/800×600.
- The FINAL milestone carries a reachability assertion that fires the player's own documented controls from a near-goal setup (the win-gate satisfied AND the player placed one short documented-input hop from the goal) and asserts the win observable becomes true — proving the level is genuinely completable by the verb without asking a generic driver to navigate the full level.
- The prose body carries a Playability section recording WIN-PATH (a concrete control sequence to the win), LEGIBILITY, ONBOARDING, and CHALLENGE/DIFFICULTY-FLOOR (which threat contests which reward/goal and where they meet on the critical path; round one reaches the test beat), a Scoring line (model + maxScore + readout, or "no score"), and a Level Ladder line (the LEVEL_ORDER + per-level escalation lever, or "one level") — the playability content that used to live in the separate PLAN.md is now this prose section of gdd.md, plus the scope IN/OUT lines.
**Red flags:**
- The DESIGN BODY emitted as strict JSON instead of prose (the format tax: a single big gdd.json-style object for entities/mechanics/scoring/level plan, paying the 5-15% reasoning tax and the ~35% token cost for a hand-off two reasoning nodes read no better as JSON) — the design body must be markdown prose; strict JSON is reserved for the milestones tail and for blueprint.json at Harden.
- The milestones are NOT in a single parseable fenced ```json tail at the very end (no fence, multiple JSON blocks, the milestones wrapped inside a larger GDD object, prose trailing after the block, or malformed so the driver/Harden cannot parse them in one shot).
- A milestone assertion is not OBSERVABLE over window.__GAME__ (e.g. 'jump() was called', reads engine internals not exposed on __GAME__), uses an input.key absent from controls[], carries more than one expect comparator, or uses exact equals on a physics-continuous quantity that varies — reward-hackable, flaky, or un-driveable.
- Status-model contradiction: a pure-respawn / soft-fail design encodes the loseCondition observable as status==='lost' AND an assertion drives a lost->playing transition (or a catch->'lost' + respawn->'playing' pair) — the exact frog1 escalation the harness's status-legality invariant forbids; the soft reset should observe player-back-at-spawn with status staying 'playing'.
- The fail-model is left implicit: a respawn/lives game carries a maxHealth tuning with no machine-readable failModel, so the template's unconditional health bar renders a phantom HP pool the game never drives (the frog1 static-100/100 defect) — fail-model declared XOR nothing for the HUD to key off.
- The final milestone's win/reachability assertion fakes the win — sets status:'won' directly in setup, fires a magic synthetic event target instead of the documented controls[], or omits the near-goal setup and instead demands a generic driver navigate the whole tense level (re-introducing the broken-navigator failure).
- UNWIRED STANDARD REACTION (the p02 defect this fix exists to prevent): a scoring/win/contact-fail mechanic bound only to a bare scene-hook id (collect→`setupCustomCollisions`, win→`onLevelComplete`, respawn→`onPlayerDeath`) or to nothing, with `custom[]` empty and no `CollectScore`/`ScoreGateGoal`/`GoalReach`/`ContactRespawn` binding — the design reads "compose-first, no custom needed" but the standard reaction is UNWIRED, so W4 HALTs (the scene-hook is an empty seam, not a logic).
- A mechanic/entity invents a capability the archetype template lacks (or violates the scopeCut), or fewer than 3 milestones / more than 5, or the win is meet-but-never-finish (win condition satisfiable yet status never reaches 'won' — the frog1 'collected the reward and the game wouldn't end' defect).
- Meaningless score: scoringModel != none but no maxScore (an unbounded open counter), rewards not marked idempotent (re-collectible / re-credited after respawn so points can be farmed — the cw1 score bug), or no score↔win tie; OR the inverse — scoringModel == none but a score counter/readout is still declared (a vestigial number nobody uses). maxScore that is not Σ reward values, or a score-gated win whose threshold exceeds maxScore.
- Thin/short round one (the recurring cw1/ceval2 defect): the design places the entities the prompt LITERALLY named (its few collectibles + 1 enemy) in a row on ONE screen and stops — a ~30-second first-try crossing — instead of ELABORATING the theme into a long escalating level. Fails the countable floor: FEWER than 3 distinct escalating beats on the critical path, OR the threat engaged only once / parked off-path, OR difficulty does not rise across the level (later beats no harder than the teach), OR the win reachable right after the teach (no earned climax). Also thin: classification.mustPreserve dropped so round one is a mechanic demo; OR the §3.7 self-critique skipped (a thin draft returned unaudited). The 'too simple' defect — reframed as thin/short, NOT 'only one level'.
- A forced multi-level ladder: the prose Level Ladder defaults LEVEL_ORDER to multiple entries (or one-level-per-milestone) when the prompt did NOT ask for multiple levels — manufacturing stages instead of enriching the ONE level; OR an explicitly-asked-for ladder sprawls past 5 milestones / reintroduces a scope-cut system / adds new code per level instead of config+placement diffs. (NOTE: a single rich level with LEVEL_ORDER:['Level1Scene'] is CORRECT, not a deficiency.) Or a WORLD extent hard-coded to a free 960×540/800×600, OR a long §3.5 path CAPPED at one viewport (bounds = 432×768 while the path spans multiple screens) instead of a MULTIPLE of the viewport width with the camera following the player (§4.6 viewport-vs-world).

## Harden (harden-blueprint)
**Artifact:** spec/blueprint.json (the COMPLETE frozen winnable design — the strict-JSON machine boundary)
**Purpose:** The PRODUCER of the frozen design the whole build binds to: W2 merges its complete config + materializes src/levels/<level>.json from its layout, W4 builds the custom[] delta VERBATIM (HALTing on any missing number), VERIFY-2 uses acceptanceCriteria + referenceSolution + declaredRanges + the carried milestone assertions as its fidelity oracle, and W3a/W3b read meta.artStyle off it. Harden formalizes W1's prose body + milestone tail into the strict blueprint — fixing every NUMBER, resolving every registry binding, proving winnable by kinematic-feasibility math, authoring referenceSolution + Given/When/Then acceptanceCriteria + declaredRanges, and CARRYING THE SUPERSET forward. It PRODUCES; it does NOT judge (no hard verdict — that is VERIFY-1). Because this is the strict-JSON boundary every downstream node binds to, any missing number / dangling binding / unfeasible traversal frozen here poisons every node after it with no recovery.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- config is COMPLETE, drawn from the archetype's capabilities.json kind=config-key ids — every tunable the design touches is present and every key resolves to a real config-key id (no missing tunable, no invented key).
- feasibility.checks carry kinematic-feasibility math with a real per-check numbersUsed a steward can re-run BY HAND: every required traversal (gap/reach/jump-window) is compared to a computed reach/jump/window value, and the comparison is TRUE at the recorded numbers — no decidable check left with empty/decorative numbersUsed.
- layout is concrete — coords/routes/timings for every spatial element — and bounds = the WORLD: the viewport for a single-screen level OR an integer MULTIPLE of the 432-wide viewport for a longer scrolling level (read the viewport from the template gameConfig.screenSize — currently 432×768 portrait — NEVER a hard-coded pixel size); every coordinate falls inside bounds.
- A referenceSolution gives an ordered sequence of the DOCUMENTED controls that reaches the win observable AND engages every threat (engagesEveryThreat true, consistent with the steps), and coupling[] puts at least one threat region on EVERY path to each contested reward/goal (no threat-free undesirable solution) — both the winnable proof and the real-decision proof on the coordinates.
- acceptanceCriteria are Given/When/Then over window.__GAME__, each 1:1 ID-linked to its gdd milestone assertion (assertionId), with exactly one comparator; the terminal/win AC's given places the player ONE short documented-input hop from the goal with the gate satisfied (near-goal precondition), NEVER a full-level navigation (the full-crossing proof lives in referenceSolution).
- declaredRanges supplies a [min,max] band for every tunable AND every placement coordinate, and the proven properties (kinematic feasibility + threat-on-path) hold at BOTH endpoints of every band — no range that makes the level unwinnable, soft-locked, or breaks a coupling at an extreme.
- Every binding is RESOLVED to a real capabilities.json id of the right KIND honoring its roles, compose-first: config-a-builtin → compose registered ids → custom[] ONLY for a genuine gap; every "$custom:<id>" reference has a matching custom[] manifest entry (no over-declared custom[] that a resolving id or composition already covers).
- STANDARD REACTIONS RESOLVE TO REGISTERED LOGICS, carried in the `systems[]` bucket (the 2026-06-14 kind=system tier): every mechanic touching `score`/win/contact-fail resolves to a registered `CollectScore`/`ScoreGateGoal`/`GoalReach` (`kind=system`, emitted into `blueprint.systems[]` as `{ref,params}`) or `ContactRespawn` (`kind=behavior` on the enemy's `behaviors[]`) — NOT left as a bare scene-hook id and NOT hand-rolled in `custom[]` (a `custom[]` for a standard reaction is over-declaration). `custom[]` is now a composition of registered logics + genuine novelty only; the params (`rewardKind`/`threshold`/`effectEvent`) carry the per-game specifics, never a hard-coded literal.
- Score hardening when meta.scoringModel != none: meta.maxScore == Σ of layout reward values (finite + reachable), the credit flow is one-shot + respawn-safe (no respawn→re-credit edge), and any score-gated win has gate-threshold ≤ maxScore; when scoringModel == none the blueprint surfaces NO score.
- The SUPERSET is carried forward so the asset + QA nodes find it ON the blueprint: meta (incl. artStyle, failModel, scoringModel, maxScore), entities[], and each milestone's assertions[] are all present, not dropped on the way from gdd.md.
- A PRODUCER SELF-CRITIQUE was run BEFORE emit: audit every traversal feasible / the referenceSolution passing through >=3 DISTINCT contested decisions on a path beyond one screen with rising difficulty + an earned climax / every binding resolved / status-coherent — each marked PASS/FAIL WITH the number, and every FAIL FIXED by HARDENING (raise a number, re-place a threat, add a contest), NEVER by weakening.
- The blueprint is strict-JSON-valid against harden-blueprint/blueprint.schema.json (all required fields present + correctly typed, no comments/trailing commas/single quotes).
**Red flags:**
- A dangling binding: an id that resolves to nothing in capabilities.json, or a "$custom:<id>" reference with no matching custom[] entry — the build binds to a phantom capability.
- A missing number the executor (W2 materialization or W4 custom[]) would have to INVENT — a coordinate, cycle, threshold, or reward value left unfrozen.
- SPAWN-OFF-PLATFORM / wrong platform-coordinate convention (the p02 void-fall defect): `playerSpawn.x` falls OUTSIDE the start platform's span `[x, x+width]` (or `y` floats above its top) so the player spawns over the void → infinite respawn soft-lock at level start; OR platform `x/y` authored as CENTER instead of the TOP-LEFT corner the SDK/W2/feasibility-math expect (`platform_end = x+width`, `cx = x+width/2`) — every platform then renders shifted by half its size and detaches from the spawn/reward coords. A blueprint VERIFY-1 should catch via a spawn-on-ground re-derivation; in companion mode W4 HALTs on it.
- A design WEAKENED to pass: a threat deleted, the win relaxed, or a range widened to hide a break, instead of the design HARDENED — the cardinal producer sin (Harden only strengthens).
- An unfeasible traversal shipped: a gap > the computed reach (or a passable window computed ≤ 0) marked clearable — a kinematic lie the feasibility math should have caught.
- Empty/decorative numbersUsed on a decidable feasibility check (a comparison asserted in prose, not decided by arithmetic).
- Over-declaring custom[] when a resolving capabilities.json id or a composition of registered ids already covers the need (custom is the last resort, not the first).
- meta.artStyle / entities[] / a milestone's assertions[] DROPPED instead of carried forward — the asset or QA node then can't find its oracle on the blueprint.
- JUDGING the design — emitting a hard DESIGN_PASSED/FAILED verdict or a DESIGN_REVIEW.md — that is VERIFY-1's job; the producer produces, it does not adjudicate.

## VERIFY-1 Design (verify-1-design)
**Artifact:** spec/DESIGN_REVIEW.md (the verdict + per-criterion math trail — VERIFY-1 produces NOTHING the build binds to)
**Purpose:** VERIFY-1 READS the HARDEN-produced blueprint.json and JUDGES it — it does NOT author it. It RE-DERIVES the feasibility math + threat-on-path + the substantial-level floor + the dangling-reference check + the score proofs + status coherence + ranges-safe, and writes the verdict to DESIGN_REVIEW.md. By the verify-node law it CREATES NOTHING the build binds to: removing this node entirely must still leave a buildable blueprint (Harden already produced it). A broken design leaked past here is expensive — every downstream node binds to it — so this is the cheapest place to CATCH a broken design; but the catching is a JUDGEMENT over a produced artifact, never a re-authoring of it.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- The verdict is EARNED by RE-DERIVED math, not deferred to Harden's claim: VERIFY-1 re-runs the feasibility arithmetic itself from each feasibility.checks[].numbersUsed and the comparison must HOLD (every required traversal vs the computed reach/jump/window), so a steward re-running by hand reaches the same verdict. A PASS over empty/decorative numbersUsed on a decidable criterion is not earned.
- Threat-on-path / no-undesirable-solution is RE-DERIVED from coupling[] against the coordinates: every contested reward/goal has at least one threat region intersecting EVERY path to it (winnable-alone or no-shortcut-alone is insufficient — both directions re-checked on the numbers).
- The SUBSTANTIAL-LEVEL FLOOR is re-checked on the referenceSolution: it PASSES THROUGH >=3 DISTINCT contested decisions, on a path well beyond a single screen, with LATER beats MEASURABLY HARDER than the teach (a wider gap / a tighter window / a faster-denser threat the numbers show) ending in an EARNED climax — a countable RELATION, never a genre constant; a thin/short first-try crossing (no threat engaged, or one trivial threat then the win) FAILS as "too simple".
- The dangling-reference CHECK is re-run: every binding resolves to a real capabilities.json id of the right kind, and every "$custom:<id>" has a custom[] entry — a leaked phantom binding is DESIGN_FAILED.
- The SCORE proofs are re-derived when meta.scoringModel != none (§4.5): score-bounded (maxScore finite + reachable + == Σ reward values, score ≤ maxScore carried into an AC), score-idempotent (no respawn→re-credit edge in the frozen flow), score-coheres (a score-gated win's threshold ≤ maxScore with that reward-set reachable); when scoringModel == none, no vestigial score is surfaced.
- The win/lose/RESPAWN state machine is re-checked for coherence with the immutable terminal status model: 'won'/'lost' are terminal sinks, a recoverable fail is NON-terminal on a DISTINCT observable (lives decrement, or player-back-at-spawn while status stays 'playing'), and 'lost' is reserved for a genuine game-over — never a catch->'lost' paired with a respawn->'playing' for the same mechanic.
- declaredRanges is re-checked SAFE at BOTH endpoints: the proven properties (kinematic feasibility + threat-on-path) hold at min AND max of every band, so VERIFY-2's perturbation can never wrongly fail a faithful build.
- DESIGN_REVIEW.md records the verdict + per-criterion PASS/FAIL math trail (the re-derived arithmetic shown, not asserted) + the residual SUBJECTIVE calls surfaced for the human eye (reads-tense / fantasy-strong-enough) rather than silently rubber-stamping them.
- The bounded ≤2 STABILIZE-edit STRENGTHENS ONLY (raise a number / re-place a threat / tighten a range) and a STRUCTURAL defect (status-model contradiction, threat decoupled, a thin floor, a dangling binding) is DESIGN_FAILED and ROUTED BACK to Harden/W1 — never patched in place, never weakened to pass. VERIFY-1 CREATES NOTHING the build binds to (the verify-node law: removing it leaves a buildable blueprint).
**Red flags:**
- Rubber-stamped math: a criterion marked PASS but the feasibility.checks are missing, have empty/decorative numbersUsed, or the recorded arithmetic does not actually support the comparison (a gap > d_max marked PASS, a passable window computed ≤ 0, a divide-by-zero/degenerate value not flagged) — a verdict READ from Harden's claim or asserted in prose rather than RE-DERIVED from the numbers.
- A stabilize-edit that WEAKENS the design: a threat deleted, a win relaxed, an AC softened, or a range widened to hide a break — instead of STRENGTHENING (raise a number, re-place a threat, tighten a range) or, for a structural defect, DESIGN_FAILED and routed back. The verifier never weakens.
- Role confusion: VERIFY-1 claims to AUTHOR/produce the blueprint (writes blueprint.json, fills a missing number, resolves a binding, invents a coordinate) — VERIFY-1 JUDGES, Harden PRODUCES; anything the build binds to that only VERIFY-1 created violates the verify-node law (removing the node must still leave a buildable blueprint).
- A thin/short crossing passed as substantial: the referenceSolution reaches the win engaging NO threat, OR only ONE trivial threat then ends, OR with FEWER than 3 distinct beats / no measurable rising difficulty (later beats no harder than the teach) / no path beyond one screen — the cw1/ceval2 shape rubber-stamped instead of DESIGN_FAILED back to Harden.
- The dangling-reference check MISSED: a binding that resolves to nothing in capabilities.json, or a "$custom:<id>" with no custom[] entry, passed as DESIGN_PASSED — a phantom binding the build then resolves to nothing.
- Status-model incoherence shipped as DESIGN_PASSED: a milestone AC asserts status==='lost' on a RECOVERABLE catch while another defines respawn lost->playing for the same mechanic (the frog1 defect) — an internally contradictory, unbuildable flow re-check should have FAILED.
- Terminal/win AC given authored at spawn (forcing the full tense crossing) instead of one hop from the goal with the gate satisfied (the frog1 F4 defect) passed unchecked; or declaredRanges with an endpoint that breaks winnability/coupling waved through; or an AC that reduces to 'feels balanced/works correctly' accepted.
- Unbounded/farmable score passed as DESIGN_PASSED: scoringModel != none with no maxScore (or maxScore != Σ reward values), a respawn→re-credit edge left in the frozen flow (the cw1 farming bug), or a score-gated win whose threshold exceeds maxScore; OR scoringModel == none yet a vestigial score counter surfaced — the §4.5 score proofs skipped or rubber-stamped.
- DESIGN_REVIEW.md missing the per-criterion PASS/FAIL math trail (verdict with no shown arithmetic) or silently rubber-stamping the residual subjective calls instead of surfacing them for the human eye.

## W2 Scaffold (w2-scaffold)
**Artifact:** STRUCTURE.md + index.json + **src/levels/<level>.json** (the architecture map + asset-slot manifest + the runtime LEVEL DATA, at the project root, atop the copied building project)
**Purpose:** W4 reads STRUCTURE.md as the per-file CONTRACT for the `custom[]` delta it authors (no longer the whole level — the SDK builds the level from the materialized data); W3a/W3b+the Preloader+W4 read index.json as the single key->dims slot map so nobody re-scans public/; the generic `DataLevelScene` reads **src/levels/<level>.json** to instantiate the ENTIRE level (platforms/spawn/goal/rewards/threats/bound behaviors/effects/timer) with zero per-game code. If the level data drifts from `blueprint.layout`+bindings, the SDK builds the wrong geometry; if STRUCTURE.md mislabels a file or omits a frozen-blueprint number, W4 builds the wrong custom shape; if index.json drifts from the entities/assetList it derives from, W3a/W3b generate the wrong art and the entity-count assertions VERIFY-2 runs go unanchored.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- index.json is strict-JSON-valid against index.schema.json: top-level archetype (matching the chosen template lane) + assetsDir + slots[]; every slot has slot/type/path/width/height/status, type is one of the enum, status is 'pending'|'placeholder' (W2 does not write 'generated'), and animation slots carry frames[] while non-animation slots omit it — slot width/height are PER-FRAME dims (the Preloader's frameWidth/frameHeight), never the sheet width (a frames:[idle,run,jump] slot at 64×64 yields a 192-wide strip on disk).
- slots[] is exactly the UNION of the upstream assetList[] and every entities[].assetSlot — no slot is invented, none is dropped; entity-backed slots carry entityIds[] provenance and each slot key is unique snake_case. An empty union legitimately yields slots:[] rather than fabricated rows.
- Every src file STRUCTURE.md names actually exists in the copied tree, and every gameplay file W4 must fill is named and marked TODO-W4 (or [M# DONE] on a re-scaffold) so the fill-in points are unambiguous; engine/KEEP files are distinguished from the to-be-written ones.
- STRUCTURE.md transcribes the FROZEN blueprint numbers it depends on verbatim (controls table from controls[], entity->file->behavior->assetSlot map, geometry/spawns/win-lose observables from the blueprint) without re-deriving or rounding them — it is provenance for W4, never a fresh design.
- The Test hook section documents window.__GAME__ as exposed in src/main.ts per the template contract (universal fields + archetype extras + snapshot()/commands), and the State & Event Map ties score/status to the registry and to the win/lose observables W4 will set — so the QA assertions have a named anchor.
- The Notes section records every scaffold decision that diverges from a clean default — dropped config keys (not in the archetype schema), archetype fallback, empty assetList, screenSize-vs-world-bounds mismatch — and these are mirrored in MEMORY.md.
- gameConfig.json merge is faithful: gdd/blueprint config values land in the correct archetype sub-object as {value,...} (template defaults for unset keys preserved), infra groups (screenSize/debugConfig/renderConfig) untouched, and controlsHelp populated verbatim from controls[] as a plain {input,action} array.
- gameConfig carries the runtime legibility seam VERBATIM from the spec: failModel (blueprint.meta.failModel, falling back to gdd.meta.failModel; absent → template default recorded in MEMORY.md, never invented) and objective (winCondition.description) land as plain values, so the shipped HUD renders ONLY the resource the chosen fail-model drives (health bar iff 'health', lives readout iff 'lives', no depleting-resource widget for 'respawn'/'none') and the TitleScreen/HUD surface the one-line goal — an inherited player.health field is not license to render a health bar.
- src/levels/<level>.json is materialized as a faithful VERBATIM projection of blueprint.layout + the capability bindings into the LevelData shape (one file per LEVEL_ORDER entry): bounds/playerSpawn/backgroundColor; platforms[] (x/y = TOP-LEFT corner) from layout.platforms[]; player {assetSlot, behaviors {ref,params}, anim}; goal; rewards[] (each {id,x,y,kind,role,entityKind,assetSlot,w,h,behaviors incl any "$custom:<id>"}); threats[] (cycleMs/activeMs/telegraphMs + shape/columnHeight/barWidth from the threat's CyclicHazard {ref,params}); effects[] verbatim; **systems[]** (the registered `kind=system` bindings `{ref,params}`) projected VERBATIM from `blueprint.systems[]` exactly like effects[] — the SDK `DataLevelScene` constructs each registered logic (`CollectScore`/`ScoreGateGoal`/`GoalReach`) from them, and a moving-enemy threat carries `ContactRespawn` in its `behaviors[]`; hitTimePenalty from blueprint.config.hazardTimePenalty IF declared else 0 (the gap noted in MEMORY.md, NEVER invented) — every value carried from the blueprint, never a coordinate/cycle/binding invented; strict-JSON-valid.
- gameConfig.shell.hud is the blueprint's shell.hud spec projected VERBATIM (the whole shell section → gameConfig.shell) — a PROJECTION of fields already declared (failModel→resource, scoringModel/maxScore→score readout, objective→banner), never a hand-authored second copy; ABSENT in the blueprint → omitted (the SDK composer's deriveDefaultHud() reproduces the same defaults), never fabricated.
**Red flags:**
- index.json drift: a slot exists for an entity that has no assetSlot (or vice-versa an entities[].assetSlot has no row), a duplicated slot key, an 'animation' slot missing frames[], or status already set to 'generated' — corrupting the W3a/W3b generation set and the VERIFY-2 entity-count anchor.
- STRUCTURE.md names a file that is not in the tree (or omits a gameplay src file), or describes BEHAVIOR/level layout/entity logic instead of just the file responsibility contract — that is W4's job and leaks design latitude into a node that should only map the shape.
- A blueprint number is paraphrased, rounded, or quietly altered in STRUCTURE.md instead of transcribed verbatim (e.g. a spawn coord, patrol bound, or gap width), so W4 builds a different game than the one frozen at VERIFY-1.
- Reporting BUILD-HEALTH success on a red build, or making it green by stubbing out / deleting template files or tightening tsconfig.json rather than fixing the actual scaffold-level cause; alternatively writing game logic / level scenes (overstepping into W4).
- An invented gameConfig field or a dropped config key with no Notes/MEMORY.md record, infra groups (screenSize/debugConfig/renderConfig) mutated, or controlsHelp wrapped as {value,...} instead of a plain {input,action} array — breaking the on-screen how-to-play and the engine contract.
- A depleting health bar shipped on a respawn/lives/none game (the frog1 static-100/100 defect, reproduced as nv1's frozen 1/1 bar), or a runtime with no on-screen objective while the spec carries winCondition.description — the scaffold carried HOW-TO-PLAY (controlsHelp) but dropped WHAT-TO-DO and the fail-model the HUD must key off.
- src/levels/<level>.json drift from the frozen blueprint: a platform/spawn/goal/reward/threat coordinate paraphrased, rounded, or INVENTED (e.g. a hazard cycleMs not in the blueprint, a hitTimePenalty conjured when blueprint.config.hazardTimePenalty is absent instead of 0+a MEMORY.md gap note), a binding {ref,params} altered, or the file omitted/non-JSON — the SDK then instantiates a different game than the one frozen at VERIFY-1. Or writing level GEOMETRY into a .ts instead of this data file (that geometry is data the SDK consumes, never code).
- gameConfig.shell.hud fabricated when the blueprint declares no shell.hud (instead of omitting it and letting deriveDefaultHud() derive defaults), or it re-encodes/contradicts the blueprint fields it should merely project (a score readout on a scoringModel:none game, a resource widget disagreeing with failModel).

## W3a Art Direction (w3a-art-direction)
**Artifact:** asset-prompts.json (palette + one creative prompt per non-audio slot)
**Purpose:** W3b's `gen/generate_assets.py` reads asset-prompts.json and sends each creative prompt VERBATIM to the image model (the python no longer invents prompts — it only wraps a given prompt with per-type technical scaffolding). So the entire creative-direction + color-taste burden lives HERE: this node is the single home of prompt-craft and palette taste in the pipeline. If a prompt names a sibling's subject the theme bleeds across assets (the "everything is a mecha" defect); if the palette gives two roles one color or a flat figure/ground, entities read alike in-game. Because W3b transmits these prompts unchanged and there is NO placeholder fallback, a bad prompt here becomes baked-in art with no recovery short of a re-run.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- EXACTLY one prompt per NON-AUDIO slot in index.json, in index.json ORDER — none skipped, none added, none reordered (audio slots are not generated and carry no prompt). The prompts[] count equals the non-audio slot count.
- Each prompt names ONLY its own subject (drawn from THIS slot's description, or the entityIds->gdd.entities[] fallback) — it contains NO other asset's subject noun. This is the anti-theme-bleed bar: a ground/tile prompt prompts a ground surface, a spark prompts a spark, never the player/hero subject.
- Each prompt embeds the GLOBAL style/mood (from meta.artStyle, stated the same way across the set so the whole set is one family) AND uses THIS asset's palette-role color, named in the prose and matching the hex committed in palette.roles.
- Each prompt carries NO technical/dimensional/negative text — no canvas size, no sprite-grid layout, no "seamless", no "transparent background", no "no text/no watermark" (W3b appends all of that deterministically per type).
- palette has a DISTINCT color per role (no two roles share a hex), as a cohesive set with figure/ground separation (player/interactives contrast the background in value or hue) and hazard salience (hazards get the reserved high-chroma warm accent; collectibles a distinct reward accent); same-role instances share a color; roles no slot uses are omitted.
- The MEASURED figure/ground floor holds: `ground` vs `background` ≥ **1.5 WCAG relative-luminance contrast** (the two largest screen masses in different value bands). SYMMETRIC — a dark-on-dark palette fails it exactly as a light-on-light one does. The gen post-hook recomputes it and HALTs below it (exit 6 + `public/assets/palette-readability.json`), so this is a hard floor, not a style choice. Additionally: the player reads against BOTH background and ground (value OR hue), and no reserved accent (collectible/hazard/goal hue) is reused as the background color.
- asset-prompts.json is strict-JSON-valid in the pinned shape ({palette:{name,roles:{role->hex}}, prompts:[{slot,prompt}]}), with each prompts[].slot EXACTLY matching an index.json slot key; the node writes no src/** or spec/** and runs no generator (that is W3b).
**Red flags:**
- A tile/spark/background/collectible prompt that names the PLAYER or hero subject (or any sibling's subject noun) — the theme-bleed bug this node exists to prevent.
- Two roles sharing a color, or a flat palette with no figure/ground separation / no hazard salience (background not receding, hazards not the reserved warm accent) — entities will read alike in-game.
- A VALUE-COLLAPSED palette: `ground` and `background` in the same value band (the dark-on-dark / light-on-light trap, < 1.5 WCAG contrast), or a reserved accent hue spent on / saturating the background, or a background prompt authored busy/high-detail/high-contrast so it competes with gameplay, or a player/interactive prompt authored as a dark "silhouette". The readability defect the deterministic gate (exit 6) now blocks; pf-p04's near-black-on-near-black "Neon Heist" is the canonical instance.
- A slot skipped, an extra prompt invented, or the prompts[] out of index.json order; a prompts[].slot that does not match any index.json key.
- Technical/dimensional/negative bits baked into a creative prompt (canvas size, grid, "seamless", "transparent background", negatives) — W3b owns those; their presence double-applies or fights the deterministic scaffolding.
- A FABRICATED subject when a slot's description (and its entityIds fallback) is missing — the node should HALT with NEEDS_SLOT_DESCRIPTION:<slot>, never invent a subject; a prompt that merely echoes the global style onto a slot with no real subject is the same defect.

## W3b Assets (w3b-assets)
**Artifact:** ASSETS.md (+ public/assets/* files + _provenance.json + index.json slot path/status write-back)
**Purpose:** W3b runs `gen/generate_assets.py --prompts asset-prompts.json`, which generates a REAL file for every non-audio slot (gemini-3.1-flash-image + a local offline chroma key), writes the audio placeholder WAVs, the ASSETS.md manifest, and writes back each slot's path+status. W4 references the frozen slot KEYS (never W3b's bytes) and the template Preloader loads each filled slot by type; VERIFY-2's screenshot/VLM verdict reads the rendered art. Real generation is MANDATORY — there is NO placeholder/greybox fallback: a missing key/deps (exit 5), a missing/unreadable asset-prompts.json (exit 4), or a value-collapsed palette that fails the readability floor (exit 6 — ground vs background < 1.5 WCAG contrast, the deterministic figure/ground tripwire) is a HALT, not a degrade. If a slot has no real file, or the file dims/format don't match the loader call (e.g. an animation strip that doesn't tile into frames.length cells), the game fails to load that texture or VERIFY-2's screenshot becomes meaningless — so coverage + on-disk fidelity are what matter, not art quality.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- EVERY non-audio index.json slot is GENERATED (a real file on disk, status:'generated') — there is NO placeholder/greybox image floor. Audio slots carry the placeholder WAV (status:'placeholder'). One ASSETS.md row per slot, slotCount tallies sum to total; an empty slots:[] case is the explicit 'no slots; boots on programmatic shapes' note, never fabricated rows.
- Each generated row points at a real file under assetsDir whose on-disk format matches the loader call for its type: PNG (RGBA for sprites/animation/tileset, fully opaque incl. effect regions for backgrounds), WAV for audio; and the file's real pixel dimensions equal the slot's width/height — for type:'animation', a horizontal strip of exactly frames.length uniform width×height cells (PER-FRAME dims; e.g. width:64,height:64,frames:[idle,run,jump] ⇒ a 192x64 file).
- The visible content fills ~CONTENT_FILL (≈0.90) of the slot box UNIFORMLY across cut-outs (a shared scale, so relative sizes read correctly in-game — the fit_content_to_slot bar); a slot whose content/slot aspect mismatch exceeds AR_FLAG (1.6×) is LETTERBOXED and FLAGGED (ar_flag), never anisotropically distorted to fill.
- index.json write-back mutates ONLY path/status (and optionally refined width/height) per slot: every slot KEY, row order, archetype, and assetsDir are byte-identical to W2's frozen file, and the row's status matches the ASSETS.md status for that slot.
- Verify-then-claim governs every row (ASSETS.md === bytes): each asserted property (dims, opacity, alpha, frame tiling) was read back from the shipped file, not asserted from intent; the manifest pair (index.json row + ASSETS.md row) state the same per-frame dims. Real sprites carry NO baked text/labels/watermarks in player-visible pixels — names live ONLY in ASSETS.md/_provenance.json provenance.
- The header records mode:gemini + the model id; if any non-audio slot failed to generate (per-slot non-fatal, left 'pending'), it is recorded + escalated in ASSETS.md Notes + MEMORY.md (NOT degraded to a placeholder). Every row's description traces to its index.json slot; W3b touches no src/** or spec/** and invents no slot/key.
- Each row carries a correct, type-specific loaderHint (load.image for sprite/image/tileset/background, load.spritesheet with frameWidth/frameHeight for animation, load.audio for audio) consistent with where the file was actually written.
**Red flags:**
- A placeholder/greybox image written as a FALLBACK for an image slot (the floor is removed) — or the node degrading to placeholders / silently producing nothing on a missing key/deps (exit 5) or missing asset-prompts.json (exit 4) instead of HALTing + escalating.
- An asset stretched beyond AR tolerance (anisotropically distorted to fill the slot) instead of letterboxed-and-ar_flag'd; or non-uniform fill across cut-outs so relative in-game sizes look off (the "ratios look off" defect).
- On-disk file contradicts the manifest/loader: animation strip whose pixel width != frames.length × frameWidth (won't slice into clean cells), a sprite with no alpha channel, an opaque-claimed background that is transparent, or dims that don't match the slot's width/height.
- A slot KEY renamed/reordered/added/removed in index.json, or W3b wrote into src/**, spec/**, or invented a tilemap/level JSON — breaking the parallel-safety contract W4 depends on.
- ASSETS.md and index.json disagree (a status, count, or row present in one but not the other), slotCount tallies don't sum to total, or a row asserts a dims/opacity property the bytes contradict (verify-then-claim skipped).
- A typed entity NAME / slot key / frame name / corner watermark baked into player-visible pixels (debug text riding the production frame — it also mirror-flips with facing).

## W4 Execute (w4-execute)
**Artifact:** src/** = ONLY the blueprint.custom[] delta (one IBehavior/ISceneSystem file per entry) + the ~10-line DataLevelScene shell + main.ts/LEVEL_ORDER registrations + appended MEMORY.md, atop a green `npm run build`
**Purpose:** The generic SDK already INSTANTIATES the whole level from W2's src/levels/<level>.json (platforms/spawn/goal/rewards/threats/bound behaviors/effects/timer) — so W4's ENTIRE job is the genuinely-novel custom[] delta. VERIFY-2 boots this build headless, fires input, and checks the milestone's frozen acceptanceCriteria against window.__GAME__ (incl. an isomorphic-perturbation re-run); the next W4 milestone EXTENDS this code. Fidelity-to-the-frozen-blueprint (the custom[] logic realizing the contract) and a truthful hook are what VERIFY-2 measures, so any design drift, hard-coded literal, layout coordinate baked into a .ts, or faked field here becomes an undetectable lie or a perturbation FAIL downstream.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- DATA-DRIVEN BOUNDARY: W4 writes NO platform/entity placement, NO behavior re-implementation, NO timer/camera/juice for anything that resolves in capabilities.json (the SDK builds all of it from src/levels/<level>.json) — INCLUDING the registered `systems[]` logics (`CollectScore`/`ScoreGateGoal`/`GoalReach`) and a moving-enemy's `ContactRespawn`: the standard collect→score / score-gate-win / contact→respawn reactions are SDK-built from the bindings, NOT W4's job; its output is EXACTLY the blueprint.custom[] entries (kind:'behavior'→src/behaviors/<Name>.ts implementing IBehavior; kind:'system'→src/systems/<Name>.ts implementing ISceneSystem) for the GENUINELY-NOVEL delta only + the ~10-line DataLevelScene shell (super(scene,levelData); registerCustomSystem/registerCustomBehavior the custom ids) registered in main.ts + LEVEL_ORDER. A coordinate written into a .ts (it belongs in the level data, W2's job) or a layout/behavior re-encoded as code is a boundary violation. Empty custom[] ⇒ ONLY the shell (CORRECT when the standard reactions all resolve to registered systems[]/behaviors[]; an UNWIRED standard reaction reaching W4 is an upstream Harden/W1 defect and W4's HALT is the correct response, never invent the wiring).
- Blueprint-verbatim custom logic: each custom[] entry realizes its frozen contract over the real SDK seams (reads scene.player/scene.decorations tagged __kind/__id/__type/scene.goalSprite, sets score via utils.setScore, wins via scene.onLevelComplete(), fires juice via scene.fireEffect) at the blueprint's real interaction distances/thresholds — config tunables pulled VERBATIM from blueprint.config, never a template default; no number nudged because it 'looks better'.
- The REAL mechanic is implemented, not a shortcut to the assertion: collect/score fires on a genuine physics overlap at the blueprint's real interaction distance, the win/lose/RESPAWN flow triggers at the exact frozen seam — and a recoverable respawn returns the player to a LIVE, CONTROLLABLE state (every layer the death funnel latched is reset, including the entity's state machine; the documented controls drive the player again after the handler) — and window.__GAME__ (score via the registry, status at the real seam, player/entities live off the scene) reflects real live state — nothing special-cased, teleported via setState, or tuned to the harness reach radius.
- KEEP-boundary respected: only NEW custom[] files (src/behaviors/<Name>.ts, src/systems/<Name>.ts) + the DataLevelScene shell + main.ts/LEVEL_ORDER registrations are written; the SDK engine files are untouched — Base*.ts, DataLevelScene.ts, level-data.ts, custom-registry.ts, behaviors/* (incl. registry.ts), ui/*, utils.ts, hook.ts, src/levels/<level>.json (W2's), and the spec/blueprint — customization is solely the custom[] delta against the IBehavior/ISceneSystem interfaces.
- Build is genuinely green (`npm run build` exit 0 via scoped root-cause fixes), with no template/engine file deleted or stubbed, no tsconfig loosened, and no failing code commented out to force the pass.
- On a missing/contradictory blueprint number the node HALTs: it records `[Mk] blueprint-gap: <field>` in MEMORY.md, returns status:'failed' with reason 'blueprint underspecified: <field>', writes NO src/** for that milestone, and leaves the project at its prior green state — never inventing a value to proceed.
- Scope is exactly this milestone: no future-milestone code, no mechanic the blueprint didn't specify; cumulative milestones EXTEND prior files rather than recreate them.
- MEMORY.md is appended with terse typed one-liners (quirks, where score/status live, overlap guards, respawn details, build fixes) and STRUCTURE.md TODO-W4 rows are ticked — the handoff VERIFY-2 and the next milestone read.
**Red flags:**
- Inventing a missing blueprint number (a coordinate, speed, period, or respawn target) or silently substituting a template default instead of HALTing — the original-sin failure this node-class exists to prevent.
- Reward-hacking the hook: code that makes __GAME__ report a value the real state lacks, an overlap tuned to the harness's DRIVE_OVERLAP_PX rather than the blueprint distance, a threat that stands down at a score threshold, or a hard-coded respawn-entrance literal — each passes the exact test but FAILS VERIFY-2's perturbation.
- Editing a KEEP/SDK-engine file (Base*/DataLevelScene/level-data/custom-registry/behaviors-incl-registry/ui/utils/hook) or mutating spec/blueprint.json or src/levels/<level>.json (W2's data) to make the build compile or the mechanic 'work'.
- Re-encoding the LEVEL as code: writing platform/entity placement, a behavior the registry already provides, or a layout coordinate into a .ts instead of leaving it to the SDK's data-driven instantiation (the data-driven boundary violated — W4 re-paying the placement cost the SDK exists to absorb, re-introducing drift from the frozen layout).
- A build contorted green by deleting/stubbing template files, loosening tsconfig, or commenting out failing code — or reporting success on a red build.
- RESPAWN mis-built as a full GAME OVER (scene restart / GameOverUIScene with status='lost') when the blueprint froze a live respawn (status stays 'playing'), or a juice effect leaking into an observed gameplay field (position/score/status).
- A live respawn that teleports the body but never returns CONTROL: the death funnel parks the entity's state machine in a terminal state ('dying') with no exit, so after the first catch the player is frozen while player-at-spawn and status==='playing' both read green (the nv1 M1 latent class — the position/status oracle cannot see it; the silent twin of respawn-as-GAME-OVER).

## VERIFY-2 QA (verify-2-qa)
**Artifact:** verify/report.M<id>.json (per-milestone, plus the verbatim VALIDATION_PASSED/FAILED marker on stdout, screenshots, and — only on a design defect — verify/escalations.M<id>.json)
**Purpose:** The orchestrator parses the verbatim marker to advance (or halt) the milestone spine, and routes a design escalation back upstream to VERIFY-1; the per-milestone report is the human's evidence trail for whether the build is a faithful, bug-free realization of the frozen blueprint. If the verdict is rubber-stamped or the marker disagrees with the recorded gates, a contorted/broken build ships as "passed" or a faithful build is false-blocked — this is the pipeline's final correctness gate, so a wrong verdict here is unrecoverable downstream.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- The marker is one of the two verbatim tokens and is CONSISTENT with the recorded gates: VALIDATION_PASSED appears iff build-health, every fidelity check, completability (when in scope), every invariant, AND perturbation.invariant===true all hold; any failed gate (incl. perturbation.invariant===false) yields VALIDATION_FAILED whose message names the failed checks' describe text. passed/marker/summary agree with the per-gate arrays.
- Every gate is backed by EVIDENCE of driving the REAL built game, not JSON narration: fidelity entries carry concrete observed values (before/after for relative comparators, e.g. player.x 130->190) read off live window.__GAME__, build-health records ready+canvasNotBlank, and screenshots exist for the end state and each failure. A relative/state comparator with no observed trace, or a pass with no booted-game evidence, is rubber-stamping.
- The isomorphic-perturbation gate actually ran (perturbation.ran===true) with concrete permutationsApplied[] of blueprint parameters reported as original->permuted within their declared ranges, and an invariant verdict with diverged[] populated on any divergence. A report missing the perturbation block, or one claiming invariance with an empty permutationsApplied[], fails to certify the build is faithful vs merely test-passing.
- Acceptance checks are driven from a PLACED precondition (the cause), never by setState-injecting the observed outcome under test; completability (on the terminal milestone) reaches the win through the real action-sequence with interim observables holding, or is explicitly scoped-out with a reason on a non-terminal milestone. A 'won' asserted right after setState({score:N}) on a door-overlap, or a green completability with no replayed interim observables, is a faked pass.
- The fix/escalation boundary is honored and recorded: any fixEdits[] touch src/** game code ONLY (never gdd.md, the blueprint, acceptance/intended-solution, the __GAME__ hook, the harness, or perturbation-grammar), fixCycles<=3 with fixOutcome set; a genuine design defect (or oracle contradiction) is ESCALATED via escalations.M<id>.json with per-contradiction evidence and routesTo VERIFY-1, not bent into a passing build. If a fix edited src/**, a regression block records the prior-milestones re-check.
- The report is the per-milestone file report.M<id>.json (NOT a clobbered report.json), schema-valid, and self-consistent: the assertions/fidelity ids match this milestone's blueprint mechanisms (no more, no less), and any failure is honestly classified (implementation-bug vs design-escalation vs verdict-correctness/broken-driver) with the trace cited.
**Red flags:**
- Rubber-stamping: a PASSED marker (or per-check 'pass') with no observed traces, no before/after values, no screenshots, or an empty/absent perturbation block — the verdict was read from JSON instead of driven from a booted window.__GAME__.
- Marker/gate inconsistency: marker says VALIDATION_PASSED while a fidelity/invariant/completability check is fail or perturbation.invariant===false; or summary/passed contradict the gate arrays (e.g. passed:true with diverged[] non-empty).
- Faked pass via injection: a precondition setState writes the very field under test (setState({score:4}) then asserts door->'won'), or completability declares reachedWin with no real action-sequence/interim observables — proving the build reads an injected number, not that the mechanic works.
- Oracle tampering or design-bending disguised as a fix: fixEdits[] modify gdd.md/blueprint/acceptance/the __GAME__ hook/the harness/perturbation-grammar, loosen a check, tune a mechanic to the harness reach radius, or force a win — instead of escalating a genuine design/oracle contradiction (the frog1 M3 lost->playing case) and reverting to keep prior milestones green.
- Verdict-correctness blindness: emitting a spurious VALIDATION_FAILED when the real cause is the broken generic driver failing to navigate a tense level or an un-drivable assertion input (should be isolation-probed/surfaced as harness gap), or clobbering a prior milestone's report by writing report.json instead of report.M<id>.json.

## Guidance (author-guidance)
**Artifact:** blueprint.guidance — coaching[] / overlays[] / worldCues[] (the `## Guidance` prose note + fenced-JSON tail, merged into blueprint.guidance, coaching at blueprint.guidance.coaching; W2 projects the whole guidance section into gameConfig.guidance — the Coaching driver reads gameConfig.guidance.coaching)
**Purpose:** A chrome-tier PRODUCER (Contract 3) that ships the game's in-game TEACHING layer — the contextual coaching tips, player-facing text overlays, and diegetic world-cues that let a first-time player LEARN THIS GAME'S LOOP at the moment it matters. It runs AFTER gameplay is frozen and BINDS to the published surface (named events · soft-state counters · spatial anchors · entity positions · observables) — never a gameplay internal number. The runtime trigger/coachmark engine fires each cue against live window.__GAME__, so any beat-less, dangling, threat-first, or never-decaying cue is teaching the player nothing (or crashing the binder) at the exact moment the surface promised a lesson. Producer, not verifier: it authors content only, never a gameplay number, never the standing HUD readout (Shell's), never the deterministic intro/objective baseline (Harden's).
**Acceptance criteria:**
- BEAT COVERAGE — every authored cue maps to a milestone or the onboarding beat (the teach order); count of cues with no beat = 0. The layer is not thin: each first-encounter beat (the core verb, each NEW mechanic at its FIRST occurrence, the win-gate state-change) has a cue OR an explicit "NONE because <genre convention the player already knows>" — a one-cue "press the keys" layer fails.
- RESOLUTION (Contract 6 invariant 5/6) — every cue's component/class (coaching → `coachmark`, the trigger engine → `trigger`) and its trigger timing (`on-ready`/`after-delay`/`on-first`/`on-state`/`on-milestone`) RESOLVE in **guidance.json** (the Guidance node's per-node JSON: components `trigger`/`coachmark` + patterns `cue-timing`/`cue-shape`), AND every trigger.observable / boundTrigger / targetEntity binds to a PUBLISHED surface handle (a named event, soft-state counter, spatial anchor, entity position, or observable dot-path). A silently-dangling trigger or unresolved component = FAIL; an HONEST `surfaceExposed:false` recorded in the gap report is OK (the gap feeds back to the surface, never a license to dangle silently).
- VERB TAUGHT SAFELY FIRST — the core-verb cue fires in a safe, low-stakes context (no threat/fail present at that beat) BEFORE any threat-contested cue; count of threat/avoid cues firing before the verb is exercised safe = 0 (the Isolation Principle — a miss while learning costs nothing).
- DECAY STORY (learned-once → gone) — every cue removes itself: a `dismissOn` on the success event (PREFERRED) OR an `on-first` one-shot OR `style.durationMs > 0` (the FALLBACK, used only when no success event resolves on the surface — a bare timer where a success event was available is the weaker form). Count of lingering cues with no removal = 0 (a standing objective is Shell's, so none is authored here).
- CLASSIFICATION CORRECT — every cue whose content/trigger is authorable from the GDD alone (only the trigger binds at merge) is an `overlay`/`coaching`; every cue that NEEDS a real entity POSITION to point at or sit on is a `worldCue`. Misclassified count = 0 (a GDD-authorable text cue tagged worldCue forces a needless serial dependency; a "point at entity X" cue tagged overlay has nothing to point at).
- NO BASELINE DUPLICATION — no `id` `intro`/`goal-banner`; no overlay restates the standing objective text (that is Shell's `intro.goalLine` / objective panel). Count = 0 — only the game-specific teaching DELTA is authored.
- COUPLING HONEST — every item records `couplingToGameplay: {needs, surfaceExposed, kind}`, and every `surfaceExposed:false` also appears in the end-of-note GAP REPORT; missing/empty coupling records = 0.
- GENERALITY — no cue's TRIGGER or SHAPE hard-codes a value only THIS game has where surface vocabulary would do (the cue BODY may name the game's nouns; the trigger timing/observable and the cue shape must be archetype-general). A trigger keyed to a literal coordinate or a baked tip lifted from another game's loop = FAIL.
**Red flags:**
- Wall-of-text intro: a single center/panel overlay dumping every control + rule at start — teaches nothing in context and duplicates Harden's baseline.
- Dangling trigger recorded as resolved: a trigger.observable / boundTrigger / targetEntity no surface section publishes (and unresolved in guidance.json), claimed `surfaceExposed:true` — a tip that never fires or a cue that crashes the binder (the silent dangle the resolution check exists to catch).
- Threat before verb: a dodge/avoid cue fires before the core verb is exercised safe — the lesson lands at high stakes where a miss costs a life.
- Overlay/worldCue misclassified — a GDD-authorable text cue called a worldCue (needless serial-after-gameplay dependency), or a "go HERE / point at entity" cue called an overlay (no position to point at) — the seam this node exists to prove, broken.
- HUD bleed (Guidance ⊥ Shell): a persistent score chip / objective panel / resource bar authored as a `coaching` entry — that is Shell's `owns`.
- Forever cue: a tip with no `dismissOn`, no `durationMs`, and not a one-shot — it sticks on screen as clutter and an immersion-breaking persistent overlay.
- A gameplay number authored (coord/speed/timing/balance), the deterministic baseline intro/objective re-authored, or a capability id invented that does not resolve in guidance.json — producer-scope and resolution violations.

## Shell (author-shell)
**Artifact:** blueprint.shell — hud[] / intro / sceneFlow / modes (the four owned keys; hud[] is the strict HudItem[] edge W2 projects into gameConfig.shell.hud via the whole-section gameConfig.shell projection, intro→gameConfig.controlsHelp/objective)
**Purpose:** A chrome-tier PRODUCER (Contract 3) that runs in PARALLEL with gameplay off the GDD ALONE — the HUD display spec, the title/how-to-play surface, the scene-flow, and the modes. It binds ONLY to GDD-declared, design-time fields (failModel · scoringModel/maxScore · winCondition.description/objective · controls[]) — none a gameplay internal — so it never waits on the gameplay node and reads NO sibling artifact. The shell is a deterministic PROJECTION: the HUD is SUBTRACTIVE (start empty, surface only declared live state, each once), the scene-flow is a projection of the fail-model, and the intro answers what/how. An invented widget (an inert resource bar on a respawn game), a dropped surface, or a wrong-format readout is a HUD the player mis-reads at a glance; a gameOver branch on a no-lose game is a scene that can never fire. Producer, not placer: it declares WHAT each surface shows + its legibility intent; the composer (UIScene/TitleScreen) owns WHERE.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- SUBTRACTIVE FLOOR=CEILING — every GDD-declared player-facing surface is represented EXACTLY once (the score iff scored; the failModel resource iff the model drives one; the standing objective iff one exists; the title surface; the scene-flow), and NOTHING is invented the GDD lacks. A dropped surface (no objective panel on a game with a standing goal; a missing control row) FAILS; an over-surfaced one (a health bar on a respawn game, a score on a `none` game, a gameOver on a respawn game) FAILS just as hard — over-surfacing is as much a defect as under-surfacing.
- failModel→WIDGET EXACT, FORMAT PINNED — the resource widget is EXACTLY the one the model drives, with format paired to the kind: `health` → `bar` on `player.health`, `format:"x/max"`; `lives` → `counter` on `lives`, `format:"int"`; `time` → `timer` on `timeRemaining`, `format:"mm:ss"`; `respawn`/`none` → NO resource widget. A `lives` shown as a bar, a `health` shown as a bare int, or a static bar on a respawn/none game (the inert-resource defect) = FAIL.
- SCORE AS PROGRESS — a `score` chip is present IFF `scoringModel != none`, formatted `x/max` when `maxScore` is finite (reads as progress toward the goal, not a bare open number); a `none` game surfaces NO score chip (the win is the readout).
- RESOLUTION (Contract 6 invariant 5/6) — every `hud[].container` RESOLVES as a **shell.json** hud-widget (`chip`/`bar`/`counter`/`timer`/`objective`) AND every `hud[].observable` resolves in the genre's observable palette (or is the `objective` sentinel); every `sceneFlow.scenes[]` entry RESOLVES as a shell.json scene (`title`/`level`/`pause`/`victory`/`complete`/`gameOver`). A container/observable/scene that does not resolve in shell.json or the palette = FAIL (the dangling-reference discipline on the chrome layer).
- howToPlay VERBATIM — `intro.howToPlay` rows are a VERBATIM copy of `controls[]` (every declared input present, none invented, none teaching-flavored), and `intro.title`/`intro.goalLine` carry `meta.title` / `winCondition.description`‖`meta.objective` — reference CHROME answering what/how, not a tip.
- gameOver BRANCH IFF LOSABLE — a `gameOver` scene is present (and `onLose` set to it) IFF the failModel can reach `status:'lost'` (`lives`→0 or `health`≤0); a respawn/none game has NO gameOver node and `onLose:null` (title → level → pause → victory/complete present always).
- SEPARATION (Shell ⊥ Guidance, no placement) — ZERO placement coordinates (x/y, anchor, pixel size, band-wrapping — the composer's) and ZERO mid-game coaching/coachmark/tip text in `intro`/`hud` (Guidance's `coaching[]`). The static title/how-to-play reference is chrome; a decaying in-level teaching cue is Guidance's — coaching bleed into the shell = FAIL.
- LEGIBILITY INTENT PER ITEM — each HUD item's `legibilityIntent` carries BOTH dimensions: what the player reads it AS (e.g. "progress to the goal", "danger resource", "standing objective") AND whether it needs a high-contrast BACKING/SCRIM on a busy portrait scene — the intent (priority + contrast need), never a pixel.
**Red flags:**
- An inert resource bar surfaced on a respawn/none game (the frog1 static-100/100 defect), or the wrong widget/format for the failModel (lives as a bar, health as a bare int) — the resource mis-read.
- A score on a `none` game, or a bounded score shown as a bare `Score: N` instead of `x/max` progress — the vestigial / un-situated counter.
- A dropped surface (objective panel omitted on a game with a standing goal; a `controls[]` row missing from howToPlay) OR an invented one (a gameOver on a respawn game) — the floor broken in either direction.
- A `hud[].container` / `hud[].observable` / `sceneFlow` scene that does not resolve in shell.json or the genre observable palette — a phantom widget/observable/scene the composer can't render.
- A placement coordinate, anchor, pixel size, or contrast rendering authored (the composer's job), or any mid-game coaching/tip/coachmark text in intro/hud (Guidance's) — separation leak / coaching bleed.
- Reading a sibling artifact (gameplay.blueprint.json / gameplay.surface.md / the Guidance section) or a gameplay internal (a spawn x, a patrol speed) to author chrome — the parallel-to-gameplay contract broken; the right move is HALT + report the separation leak, never reach inside.
- A HUD item with no `legibilityIntent`, or one missing a dimension (what-it-reads-as XOR the backing/scrim need).

## Sound (sound-author)
**Artifact:** blueprint.sound — soundPath{} / sfx[] (the `## Sound` prose note + fenced-JSON tail, written to spec/sound.fragment.json, folded into blueprint.sound; W2 projects the whole sound section into gameConfig.sound — the single shared SoundPlayer (core-contract/src/sound, mounted once per main.ts via mountSound) reads gameConfig.sound.sfx and plays each binding off the SAME `__GAME__.events` seam Guidance polls)
**Purpose:** A chrome-tier PRODUCER (ISOMORPHIC to Guidance) that ships the game's in-game SOUND/SFX layer — a best-match short sound on every gameplay MOMENT that fires an event, made coherent by one overall SOUND PATH (the sonic identity, the audio analog of meta.artStyle). It runs in PARALLEL off the frozen blueprint and BINDS each `on` to the published event vocabulary (`effects[].on` ∪ `custom[].emits[].name`) — never a gameplay internal — and SELECTS each `play` from the reused SFX menu (`sound-registry.json`) — never generates a sound. Sound ⊥ Guidance: one event name joins a coachmark + an sfx as two INDEPENDENT consumers. Producer, not verifier: it authors the sound section only, never a gameplay number, never guidance/teaching content, never an effect capability.
**Acceptance criteria:**
- SELECT, NEVER GENERATE — every `play` is a key that RESOLVES in sound-registry.json (the reused @studio/sound-kit menu); count of `play` keys not in the registry = 0. No generated, placeholder, or invented sound (there is NO generator here — SELECT from the menu or log a gap and drop the binding).
- BIND TO A REAL EVENT — every `on` is a name in the event vocabulary (`effects[].on` ∪ `custom[].emits[].name`); count of invented/dangling-but-claimed-resolved `on` = 0. An HONEST `couplingToGameplay.surfaceExposed:false` recorded in the gap report (for a needed-but-absent event) is OK — a SILENT dangle is the failure (the gap feeds back to the Gameplay surface, never a license to invent the event or reach into the internals).
- BEST MATCH — each pick is the best fit for its moment by the registry's `role`/`tags` (a pickup → `pop`, a reward → `chime`, a win → `ta-da`, an error → `oops-soft`); arbitrary/mismatched picks = 0.
- ONE COHERENT SOUND PATH — exactly ONE `soundPath{}` is stated (seeded by coreFantasy), and the picks are consistent with it (one family, not a grab-bag); the prose note explains the fit. Off-family picks = 0.
